ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, BRAYTON PURCELL'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF GLENN CRESSY. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED. BRAYTON PURCELL FAILED TO PROVIDE PROPER NOTICE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 1005(B), WHICH INCREASES THE 16-COURT-DAY NOTICE PERIOD BY 20 DAYS WHEN SERVICE IS BY MAIL TO A LOCATION OUTSIDE THE UNITED STATES. THE PROOF OF SERVICE DEMONSTRATES THAT BRAYTON PURCELL SERVED PLAINTIFF ON DECEMBER 22, 2011 BY OFFICE MAILING TO AN ADDRESS IN ECUADOR. ALTHOUGH AN EMAIL ADDRESS IS LISTED, THERE IS NO AUTHORITY ALLOWING FOR SERVICE BY EMAIL. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED
ORDER DENYING WITHOUT PREJUDICE BRAYTON PURCELL LLP'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, BRAYTON PURCELL'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF GLENN CRESSY. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: CLAUDIA J. KNAP OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #6099

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: BRAYTON PURCELL'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF GLENN CRESSY.
Appearances: Oren Noah of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) counsel for plaintiff and moving to be relieved; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of Aiken Welch, CSR #6099 (503/TLJ)

DEPT 206 ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2012 Continued To Apr-19-12 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 503

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jan-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Brayton Purcell's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Glenn Cressy is denied. Brayton Purcell failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. 1005(b), which increases the 16-court-day notice period by 20 days when service is by mail to a location outside the United States. The proof of service demonstrates that Brayton Purcell served plaintiff on December 22, 2011 by office mailing to an address in Ecuador. Although an email address is listed, there is no authority allowing for service by email. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)
